commit.message:
fix: crash on note deletion (UUID bridge on deleted Core Data object)

Root cause: selectedNote was cleared AFTER context.delete(), so SwiftUI's
observation fired between deletion and nil-assignment, accessing .id on a
deleted NSManagedObject — which bridges nil to non-optional UUID and traps.

Fix: clear selectedNote and remove from notes array BEFORE Core Data deletion.
Also guard NoteEditorView body and flushSave against deleted/orphaned notes.

v0.64.3/fix: Audit findings — 30 verified fixes across security, data integrity, and UX (#651)

## Summary

Implements 30 actionable findings from the cross-examination audit
(`docs/plans/2026-03-06-audit-5-verified.md`), organized by severity:

### High (2)
- **Offline mutations**: transient 5xx errors now retry on next drain
instead of being permanently discarded (data loss prevention)
- **Biometric IPC**: all 5 biometric commands wrapped with timeout
protection (30s for prompts, 1.5s for checks)

### Medium (10)
- Push `reminder_sent_at` only set when ≥1 push succeeds (prevents
permanent reminder loss on FCM outage)
- Cache cleared on 401/session expiry (prevents cross-user data leak in
Tauri)
- Global `MutationCache.onError` fallback — silent 4xx errors now show
toast
- `onError` added to 5 settings mutations (calendar, domain archive,
snapshots)
- `smartCallbacks` memoized in `TaskFieldsBody` (new ref every render →
stable)
- `formatDayHeader` timezone-aware (was using browser local time for
Today/Tomorrow)
- Zustand `useUIStore` persist config gets `version: 1` for future
schema migrations
- Task cache key scoped to dashboard params (filtered queries no longer
clobber cold-start cache)
- `defaultPendingComponent` + `defaultErrorComponent` added to TanStack
Router (chunk load failure detection)
- Push token persisted to `tauri-plugin-store` (survives app restart)

### Low (18)
- Dev CORS origins gated behind `!is_production`
- Push token `max_length=500` validation
- `fire_and_forget_unsync_task` circuit breaker
- `reminder_sent_at` reset on uncomplete
- No-token users no longer get reminder consumed
- Composite index for push reminder query + migration
- `data_version` lock contention: `debug` → `warning`
- IPC timeout extracted to shared `tauri-constants.ts`
- Circuit breaker half-open pattern (30s cooldown)
- Cache TTL (7 days)
- Swift `evaluateJavaScript` error logging
- Tab bar route exact + separator matching
- `clear_encryption_key` biometric gate on mobile
- `connect-src` CSP narrowed to `api.whendoist.com`
- Undo mutation `onError` with cache invalidation
- Toast `duration: 2000` override removed
- Touch targets ≥44px on mobile
- Keyboard nav logic extracted to `use-autocomplete-nav.ts`

### Skipped (7 — architectural/blocked)
- Stateless device token revocation (needs denylist design)
- Encryption key in JSON not keychain (blocked on tauri-plugin-keystore)
- Offline sync conflict detection (major feature)
- Recurring instance push reminders (needs migration)
- Native notification scheduling (needs Swift plugin)
- CSP disabled on iOS (upstream WKWebView limitation)
- CSP unsafe-inline (needs Tauri nonce support)

## Test plan
- [x] Backend: `uv run ruff format . && uv run ruff check . && uv run
pyright app/ && just test` (518 tests pass)
- [x] Frontend: `npx tsc --noEmit && npx biome check . && npm run build`
(all pass)
- [ ] Verify push reminders are retried when FCM is down (staging)
- [ ] Verify 401 clears Tauri cache (manual test on device)
- [ ] Verify biometric timeout shows error instead of hanging

fix: rollback session after enrichment/resolution errors

When enrichment fails with a DB error during flush, the SQLAlchemy
session enters PendingRollbackError state. Without explicit rollback,
all subsequent operations (humanitarian bridge, actor resolution,
corporate resolution) cascade-fail for every remaining event, wasting
~130s of Lambda time on guaranteed failures.

fix: round 5 review — seq race, broadcast aliasing, reconnect seq, decoder/event safety

CRIT-1: TickWithSeq/TickWithEvents now use tickInternal() which returns
seq atomically — prevents wrong sequence when concurrent Tick() runs.

BUG-1: Broadcast() now copies fullDiff for subsequent unfiltered clients
to prevent byte slice aliasing in the result map.

BUG-2: Reconnect() full-state path updates clientSeq after Full() returns
to account for concurrent ticks during encoding.

BUG-4: decodeFullState() adds fieldCount bounds check against remaining
buffer, consistent with decodePatch().

BUG-5: EventBuffer.Drain()/Clear() zero out retained slice elements
before truncating to allow GC of payload/slice references.

commit.message:
Fix: allow grading AI-flagged submissions by auto-clearing flag on rubric save

When a teacher grades an AI-flagged submission via rubric, the AI flag is
automatically cleared and the submission status restored. This fixes the
workflow where a teacher investigates, decides work is legitimate, and wants
to grade it without needing to manually unflag first. Adds inline banner
explaining the auto-clear behavior and contextual toast feedback.

causing e2e tests to hit 502 on early requests.

only returns 200 when the actual application is serving.

path (previously was a non-blocking warning).

commit.message:
feat: add authentication support for custom domains

Implement JWT relay authentication flow for custom domains, enabling
private visibility settings on custom domains like subdomains.

Flow:
1. User visits private custom domain (e.g., docs.acme.com/private)
2. Redirects to workspace login with customDomainRelay=true
3. After login, frontend requests domain token from backend
4. Redirects to custom domain callback with token
5. Callback sets bffless_access/bffless_refresh cookies
6. User authenticated on custom domain

Backend changes:
- Add DomainTokenService for short-lived JWT relay tokens (5 min)
- Add CustomDomainAuthService for custom domain cookies (15m/7d)
- Add CustomDomainAuthController at /_bffless/auth/* endpoints
- Add POST /api/auth/domain-token endpoint
- Update OptionalAuthGuard to check bffless_access cookies
- Update public.controller to redirect custom domains to login
- Add auth location blocks to nginx configs

Frontend changes:
- Update LoginPage to handle domain relay after login
- Enable private visibility option for custom domains in UI
- Remove outdated "cookies don't work cross-domain" restrictions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use wallet Address object instead of Address.fromString on bech32

Address.fromString() requires hex public keys, not bc1... addresses.
The wallet connector provides a pre-resolved Address object — use it
directly for all contract calls. Keep walletAddress string for display,
fee tracking, and cache keys.

- approvalService: accept Address instead of string for owner param
- contractService: pass token address string to getContract (resolves internally)
- All hooks: destructure address from useWallet, pass to service calls
- useScan: pass address.toHex() to BlockScanner for event matching
